page: Microsoft Azure NC6s_v3 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine belonging to the NCSv3 server family. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features an x86_64 Intel processor configured with 6 dedicated vCPUs. The hardware architecture provides 112.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 18.67 GB per vCPU. The system is equipped with a single GPU and 754 GB of local storage, and it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Architectural tradeoffs include the absence of advanced CPU instruction sets in the instance configuration. Flags for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512), Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), and hardware-assisted cryptography (AES, SHA_NI) are not exposed. Additionally, hyper-threading and virtualization extensions are disabled. The network configuration does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring manual allocation for external connectivity.

Given the inclusion of a dedicated GPU, substantial memory capacity, and local storage, this instance represents a specialized hardware class designed for resource-dense workloads. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it suitable for memory-intensive GPU tasks. Appropriate workloads include deep learning training, scientific simulations, and data visualization projects where datasets must reside entirely in system memory.
description: Microsoft Azure NC6s_v3 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine from the NCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 6 dedicated x86_64 Intel vCPUs paired with 112.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high ratio of 18.67 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with a single GPU and 754 GB of local storage, and it supports Premium Storage. Tradeoffs include the absence of exposed advanced CPU instruction sets like AVX, AMX, and hardware cryptography flags, as well as zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This configuration is tailored for memory-intensive GPU workloads, such as deep learning, scientific computing, and data visualization, where a substantial memory footprint is required alongside dedicated graphics acceleration.
